documentControl.purpose: Describe the architecture of the NorthWind Online e-commerce platform replacing the legacy Umbraco storefront before its end-of-support deadline and PCI-DSS v4.0 compliance gate.
documentControl.scope: Customer-facing web and mobile commerce platform, associated microservices, data stores, and third-party integrations. Excludes in-store EPOS systems and the corporate ERP.

executiveSummary.solutionOverview: NorthWind Online is the customer-facing e-commerce platform for NorthWind Retail, serving approximately 2.3 million active monthly customers across the UK. It replaces the legacy Umbraco-based storefront (deployed 2017, end-of-support 2026) with a microservices architecture running on AWS EKS in eu-west-2. The solution comprises a Next.js storefront, 14 Node.js microservices organised by bounded context, Aurora PostgreSQL for transactional data, OpenSearch for product discovery, and Redis for session state. Payments are processed via Stripe; fulfilment integrates with the existing Manhattan WMS. Peak capacity target is £30M of transactions per day during Black Friday.

businessContext.description: Umbraco 7.x reaches end-of-support December 2026 with no in-place upgrade path
Current platform cannot meet PCI-DSS v4.0 requirements without substantial re-engineering
2024 Black Friday outage (43 min) cost £4.7M revenue and triggered board mandate for resilience improvements
Mobile commerce grew from 38% to 64% of traffic in three years with no native app

inScope: Cart, checkout, and payment integration via Stripe
Order capture and integration with Manhattan WMS
outOfScope: Buy Now Pay Later integrations (deferred to Phase 2)
executiveSummary.currentState: Legacy Umbraco 7 storefront on Windows Server 2016 in NorthWind co-location DC. Monolithic .NET application; no horizontal scaling; recurring memory issues at peak. Customer database in MS SQL Server 2014 with synchronous replica.

keyDecisions.rationale: Existing AWS landing zone; team skills; better managed-DB ecosystem
keyDecisions.implication: All net-new infrastructure on AWS eu-west-2

keyDecisions.rationale: Existing team skills; corporate standard; portability
Superior JSONB support; stronger consistency; better observability
keyDecisions.implication: RDS Aurora PostgreSQL for transactional data

keyDecisions.rationale: Group-wide contract; reduces PCI scope to SAQ A-EP via tokenisation
keyDecisions.implication: Dependency on Stripe; PCI scope reduced

keyDecisions.rationale: UK GDPR and corporate data policy require customer PII in the UK
keyDecisions.implication: eu-west-2 (London) primary; eu-west-1 (Ireland) DR for non-PII only

compliance.regulatedActivityDetails: No FCA-regulated activities. Payment regulation (PSD2 SCA) satisfied by Stripe acting as the acquirer.

regulatoryRequirements.name: PCI-DSS v4.0
regulatoryRequirements.impact: Scope reduced to SAQ A-EP via Stripe Elements tokenisation

regulatoryRequirements.name: UK GDPR / Data Protection Act 2018
regulatoryRequirements.applicability: Mandatory — platform processes customer PII at scale
regulatoryRequirements.impact: DPIA completed; retention policies enforced; right-to-erasure supported

regulatoryRequirements.name: WCAG 2.2 AA
regulatoryRequirements.impact: Storefront and mobile app must meet AA

designPatterns.rationale: Independent deployment and scaling per business domain
Decouple order capture from fulfilment via Kafka choreography
CloudFront + ALB for edge caching, rate limiting, WAF
Resilience around third-party calls (Stripe, Manhattan, SendGrid)

externalIntegrations.purpose: Card payments via Stripe Elements tokenisation
Order capture and fulfilment status sync
Marketing email and customer journey orchestration

dataView.dataSovereigntyDetails: All customer PII held in eu-west-2 (London). Non-PII operational data may replicate to eu-west-1 (Ireland) for DR. No data leaves the UK/EEA.

constraints.constraint: All customer PII must remain in UK
constraints.impactOnDesign: All primary data stores in eu-west-2; DR limited to non-PII

assumptions.assumption: Stripe SLA of 99.99% holds during peak
assumptions.impactIfFalse: Card payment failures during Black Friday

assumptions.assumption: Manhattan WMS API supports projected order volume
assumptions.impactIfFalse: Order capture decoupled but fulfilment backlog

risks.riskEvent: Migration cut-over fails during peak window
risks.mitigationPlan: Phased percentage-based rollout from June 2026; full automated rollback to legacy via DNS within 5 minutes; freeze window from 1 Nov
Adyen 3DS fallback for SCA; documented manual reconciliation; status page communication runbook
Quarterly internal audit; QSA early-engagement Q2 2026; ASV scans monthly; tokenisation reduces scope

risks.riskEvent: OpenSearch cluster degrades search relevance during peak
risks.mitigationPlan: Pre-peak load test; read-replica autoscaling; relevance fallback to PostgreSQL FTS

risks.riskEvent: Aurora write capacity exhausted at peak
risks.mitigationPlan: Pre-warmed Aurora Serverless v2; read-replica usage; queue-based write absorption for non-critical writes

glossary.definition: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
PCI Self-Assessment Questionnaire A-EP — for merchants outsourcing card processing while controlling payment page
